events.text: Patient is a 55-year-old Hispanic female with a BMI of 33.2. Medical history includes obesity and hypothyroidism. Sister had breast cancer at age 50 (HER2-negative).

At age 53, patient presented with a palpable right breast mass and right axillary lymphadenopathy. Biopsy confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), right upper inner quadrant (C50.2), grade 3, ER positive (Allred 5/8), PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 60%.
At age 53, right breast core biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3. ER positive 40%, PR negative (
At age 53, mammogram showed a 3.5 cm irregular mass in the right upper inner quadrant with suspicious microcalcifications, BIRADS 5. Ultrasound confirmed matted axillary nodes.
At age 53, PET/CT staging revealed right breast mass (SUVmax 9.7), right axillary nodes (SUVmax 7.3), a single 1.2 cm right hepatic lobe lesion (SUVmax 4.8), and suspicious sternal lesion.
At age 53, de novo stage IV (cT2N2M1) HER2-positive, ER-positive breast cancer with liver and bone (sternal) metastases.
At age 53, FoundationOne CDx: ERBB2 amplification, PIK3CA C420R, TP53 G245S, FGFR1 amplification. TMB 5 mutations/Mb. MSS.
At age 53, initiated first-line THP (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days plus zoledronic acid for bone metastasis.
At age 53, baseline LVEF 62% on MUGA scan.
At age 53, after cycle 3 of THP: grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, grade 1 myalgia, and grade 2 diarrhea from pertuzumab.
At age 54, restaging CT after 6 cycles showed complete resolution of liver lesion and stable sclerotic sternal lesion (likely healing). Breast mass decreased to 1.8 cm. Near-complete response.
At age 54, docetaxel discontinued after 6 cycles. Continued maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab plus letrozole 2.5 mg daily (postmenopausal).
At age 54, LVEF 57% on surveillance echocardiogram. Asymptomatic. Continued treatment.
At age 54, PET/CT at 9 months showed no FDG-avid disease. Complete metabolic response maintained.
At age 54, patient with complete response on HP maintenance and letrozole. ECOG 0. Weight stable. Tolerating regimen well. Continue surveillance every 3 months.
At age 55, CT showed new 2.5 cm mediastinal lymph node and enlarging right axillary node. Progressive disease after 18 months on first-line therapy.
At age 55, transitioned to second-line T-DM1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.
At age 55, restaging CT after 3 cycles of T-DM1 showed decreased mediastinal lymph node (1.5 cm) and stable axillary node. Partial response.
At age 55, on T-DM1: grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 elevated AST (42), platelets stable at 140,000. Tolerable side effect profile.
At age 55, partial response on T-DM1. Plan to continue. If progression, will consider trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) as third-line therapy. Monitoring LFTs and platelets closely.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
